title: [lora_l2.c:306-411] rx_thread error backoff does not reset on state transition
description:
Completeness: Error handling completeness category.

In rx_thread (line 306-411), the error backoff at line 366 (k_sleep K_MSEC(1000)) triggers after consecutive errors. However:

1. If errors occur, then state transitions to STOPPED (line 347), the thread exits immediately
2. On restart (new rx_thread created), consecutive_errors is fresh (stack local, initialized to 0)
3. But if errors occurred just before stop, then start is called quickly, the radio hardware state may still be problematic

This is correct behavior (fresh start = fresh counter), but the design implicitly assumes hardware issues are transient. If hardware is persistently flaky, each stop/start cycle resets the error counter without any escalation.

Consider adding a module-level persistent error counter that survives stop/start cycles, or at minimum document this behavior.
close_reason: Fixed: added comment documenting that error counter is thread-local and resets on stop/start intentionally
